notes: Started problem space exploration

Identified audience: all team members and external contributors
Determined documentation depth: comprehensive with OpenAPI spec
Defined performance focus areas: indexing, queries, pagination, connection pooling
Established relationship with core-infra: both standards and documentation
Completed problem_space.md document
Created PR #171 for initial unit setup
Created BSD document
Defined problem statement: lack of centralized database documentation
Established scope: Database Design Documentation, API/DB Documentation, Pattern Documentation, Migration & Schema Management
Defined 10 measurable success criteria
Identified dependencies: core-infra, PostgreSQL, SQLC, Goose
QA review passed with no critical issues
Created user stories document
Covered all 6 BSD feature areas with 16 scenarios
Used proper Gherkin syntax (Given/When/Then)
Mapped acceptance criteria to test priorities
QA review passed with minor observations
Minor gaps: data type conventions, connection pooling/transaction patterns
Created FSD document with 24 functional requirements
Mapped all 18 user stories to functional requirements
Defined 6 functional areas covering all BSD scope items
Initial QA found critical issue: missing agent update requirement
Fixed FSD: added FR-6.5 for agent configuration updates
Fixed medium issues: SQLC validation, entity group definitions
Fixed low issues: pagination patterns, README.md updates
QA verification passed: all issues addressed
Created research.md with comprehensive technology evaluation
Evaluated 6 major areas: schema docs, ERD, OpenAPI, agent docs, migration docs, validation
Schema Documentation: Recommended custom Go script + pg_catalog (stack-aligned, markdown output)
ERD Generation: Recommended SQL → Mermaid generator (automated, version-controlled)
OpenAPI: Recommended Annot8 (Chi-native, SQLC-aware, OpenAPI 3.1) with swaggo fallback
Agent Integration: Recommended AGENTS.md-style structured markdown (industry standard, Linux Foundation)
Migration Docs: Recommended Goose native documentation with schema dump validation
Validation: Recommended custom Go script for schema-doc comparison + mmdc for diagram validation
Key insight: All recommendations minimize external dependencies, favor Go stack alignment
Key insight: Markdown is the optimal format for both human and agent consumption (4x token efficiency)
QA review found research covered only 23% of FSD requirements
Expanded research to cover all 26 functional requirements
Added Pattern Documentation research (FA-3): naming conventions, data types, connection pooling, transactions, query patterns
Naming conventions: snake_case unanimous consensus, SQLC handles snake_case → PascalCase mapping
Data types: UUID gen_random_uuid() default, TIMESTAMPTZ for timestamps, JSONB for flexible data, hybrid approach recommended
Connection pooling: pgxpool recommended for PostgreSQL, pgxpool config guidance, PgBouncer for multi-instance
Transactions: READ COMMITTED default, SERIALIZABLE requires retry logic, standard BeginTx + defer Rollback pattern
Query patterns: cursor-based pagination for large datasets, CTEs for multi-step queries, domain-organized markdown
Added Index Strategy research (FR-1.3): B-tree default, GIN for JSONB, partial indexes, extracted via pg_catalog
Added SQLC Workflow research (FR-2.3): domain-organized query files, meaningful names, CI/CD verification with sqlc diff
Added Endpoint-to-DB Mapping (FR-2.2): Handler → Service → Repository → SQLC call chain documentation
Added Error Code research (FR-2.4): RFC 9457 Problem Details, custom envelope pattern, database-to-API error mapping
Added Standardization research (FA-5): legacy pattern identification, four-phase migration, expand-contract pattern, view-based compatibility
Research now covers all 26 functional requirements (FR-1.1 through FR-6.5)
Document expanded from 650 lines to 1366 lines
QA review found 7 issues requiring fixes
Fix 1: Added Cursor vs Offset pagination performance comparison (FR-1.4/FR-3.5) - O(1) vs O(N) complexity, use case matrix, index requirements
Fix 2: Added Agent prompt template patterns (FR-6.2) - Structured Task, Schema Context, Constraint-First templates with examples
Fix 3: Added Rollback decision tree (FR-4.2) - Three scenarios (pre-deploy, post-deploy, data migration), safety checks, down function requirements
Fix 4: Added Backward compatibility alternatives (FR-5.4) - Expand-Contract, Database View, API Versioning with comparison matrix
Fix 5: Added Connection pooling parameter tuning (FR-3.3) - Environment-specific values table (dev vs prod vs multi-instance), monitoring via pool.Stat()
Fix 6: Added Legacy pattern identification alternatives (FR-5.1) - Custom Go Script, SchemaCrawler Lint, pgSchema Diff, Manual Audit with comparison matrix
Fix 7: Added Agent constraints research (FR-6.3) - Four constraint categories (tooling, schema, naming, safety) with decision trees
Document expanded from 1366 lines to 1861 lines
All 7 QA issues resolved
